stories.title: Back-to-Back Rainouts — Series Opens Tonight vs. Mets
stories.angle: Second consecutive Cubs rainout reshuffles the week; tonight's Imanaga-Senga matchup is the effective series opener; Wednesday split doubleheader; Boyd returns Thursday for Game 4

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
Back-to-back rainouts — Sunday at Wrigley, Monday at Citi Field — scrambled the Cubs' week.

The series opens TONIGHT: Imanaga vs. Senga at 6:10 PM CT. Wednesday is a doubleheader. Boyd returns Thursday. Four games in four days against a 34-43 Mets squad.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

research.facts:
Rainout — Series Schedule Reshuffled
**Facts (HIGH confidence unless noted):**
- June 22 Cubs-Mets game postponed due to rain at Citi Field (MLB.com official announcement)
- Second consecutive rainout: June 21 (Cubs-Blue Jays, Wrigley Field, makeup Aug. 6) + June 22 (Cubs-Mets, Citi Field)
- Replacement doubleheader: Wednesday June 24, Game 1 at 12:10 PM CT / Game 2 at 6:10 PM CT (1:10 PM ET / 7:10 PM ET)
- Tonight (June 23): Imanaga vs. Senga at 6:10 PM CT — sources: SI.com, Yahoo Sports (confirmed named starters)
- Matthew Boyd's next start: with big-league team Thursday (June 25) on regular rest — source: Chicago Sun-Times, Bleacher Nation (June 22)
- Cubs record: 40-37 (series-context.json)
- Mets record: 34-43 (series-context.json)
**Sources:**
- MLB.com: Cubs-Mets June 22 postponement announcement
- Chicago Sun-Times: "Cubs' series-opening game with Mets rained out, doubleheader coming Wednesday"
- CBS News Chicago, Fox Sports, Washington Post, Journal Gazette — all confirm same details
- SI.com: "Mets Announce Starting Pitchers for Cubs Series: Senga vs. Imanaga Opens Pivotal Showdown"

stories.title: Bold Take — Senga 0-5 / 9.00 ERA Is a Cubs Opportunity
stories.angle: Bold — Senga barely holding a roster spot at 9.00 ERA; Cubs are 3-0 vs. Mets this season; this is exactly the kind of series contenders must dominate

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Senga is 0-5 with a 9.00 ERA and just came off the IL.

He's not Imanaga's equal right now. He's barely holding his spot in a rotation. Cubs swept the Mets 3-0 in April. Tonight is a statement series waiting to happen.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

research.facts:
Bold Take — Senga 0-5 / 9.00 ERA
**Facts (HIGH confidence unless noted):**
- Senga 2026 record: 0-5
- Senga 2026 ERA: 9.00 (ESPN, CBS Sports, FantasyPros consistent)
- Senga recently activated from IL for this start (FantasyPros: "Kodai Senga activated from IL")
- Cubs swept Mets 3-0 in April 2026 (pipeline history)
- Senga had promising first two starts (3.09 ERA) before deteriorating significantly
**Cross-check needed:**
- Confirm exact games started: one source says "0-4," another "0-5" — most recent sources say 0-5 at 9.00 ERA; use 0-5 (MEDIUM — flag in fact-check)
- Senga "activated from IL" for this specific start (HIGH — FantasyPros confirmed)
**Sources:**
- ESPN, CBS Sports, FantasyPros (Senga stats and IL activation)
- Amazin' Avenue: Senga's early 2026 stats analysis

stories.title: Steele Resumes Throwing; Boyd Returns Thursday
stories.angle: Informative — Steele's first throw since flexor strain setback (milestone, but still weeks from return); Boyd on track to rejoin rotation Thursday June 25 (the rotation road map is clearing, slowly)

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Justin Steele picked up a baseball again Monday — his first throw since his flexor strain setback. No firm return date; best case post-All-Star.

Add Boyd returning Thursday and the Cubs' road map is finally, SLOWLY clearing.

#Cubs #MLB #CubsBaseball

research.facts:
Steele Resumes Throwing / Boyd Returns Thursday
**Justin Steele facts:**
- Resumed throwing program Monday, June 22 (HIGH — CBS Sports, CubsInsider)
- Injury history: UCL revision surgery May 2025 → medically cleared Feb. 28 → flexor strain setback late April 2026
- No firm return date
- Best case: post-All-Star break; could be fall 2026
- Flying to Chicago; working with Cubs directly on rehab
- 3+ weeks of ramp-up before throwing to live hitters
**Matthew Boyd facts:**
- Rehab start June 20 at South Bend: 4 IP, 2 H, 0 ER, 0 BB, 7 K (HIGH — Chicago Sun-Times)
- Craig Counsell confirmed: "His next outing will come with the big-league team"
- Return start: Thursday June 25 on regular rest (HIGH — multiple sources)
- Season line: 4-3 (approx), 5 starts total in 2026 due to two IL stints (knee surgery + shoulder soreness)
- Boyd was All-Star last season
**Sources:**
- CBS Sports: "Cubs' Justin Steele: Resumes throwing program"
- CubsInsider.com: "Cubs Injury Updates: Boyd Throwing Bullpen Tuesday, Steele Resuming Throwing Program"
- Chicago Sun-Times (June 22): Matthew Boyd next start confirmed with big-league club
- Bleacher Nation (June 22): Rotation lineup with doubleheader + Boyd update

stories.title: Trade Deadline — Cubs Already Talking to Mets About Peralta
stories.angle: Analysis/bold — Cubs have spoken with Mets about Freddy Peralta; Mets 34-43 likely sellers; Counsell managed Peralta 5 years in Milwaukee; sub-$3M rental, FA after season; 41 days to August 3

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
The Cubs have already talked to the Mets about Freddy Peralta.

Mets are 34-43. If they're selling, Peralta — a sub-$3M rental Craig Counsell knows from five years in Milwaukee — is exactly what this rotation needs. 41 days to August 3.

#Cubs #MLB #CubsBaseball

research.facts:
Trade Deadline — Peralta + Cubs Context
**Facts:**
- Cubs have spoken with Mets about acquiring Freddy Peralta (MEDIUM — Bleed Cubbie Blue, FanSided, Yahoo Sports / Si.com)
- Peralta owed less than $3M at deadline (MEDIUM — single source; flag for cross-check)
- Peralta is a free agent after the 2026 season
- Craig Counsell managed Peralta for 6 years in Milwaukee (HIGH — well-established public fact)
- Cubs rotation: 27th in ERA (4.71) through ~75 games (HIGH — multiple outlets citing same figure)
- August 3 trade deadline: 41 days away (HIGH — calculated from June 23 to Aug 3)
- Mets record: 34-43 (HIGH)
- Cubs GM Carter Hawkins confirmed rotation upgrades as top deadline priority (HIGH — Yahoo Sports / The Athletic)
- Other targets mentioned: Tarik Skubal, Joe Ryan, Reid Detmers, Sandy Alcántara (MEDIUM)
**Sources:**
- Bleed Cubbie Blue: "Could the Cubs bolster the rotation by trading for Freddy Peralta?"
- FanSided: "This Cubs-Mets trade for Freddy Peralta would give Chicago the edge over the Dodgers"
- Yahoo Sports: "Cubs Prioritize Starting Pitching Upgrades Ahead of August 3 Trade Deadline"
- CBSSports: Trade deadline candidates including Peralta

stories.title: Pre-Game Hype — Imanaga Takes the Mound at 6:10 PM CT
stories.angle: Hype — Imanaga's rain-delayed start finally happens tonight; he's looking to bounce back from a 4-6 record; Senga has a 9.00 ERA; Cubs have swept this team before

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
First pitch tonight: 6:10 PM CT at Citi Field.

Imanaga takes the ball in what should've been Monday's series opener. He's looking to flip a 4-6 record. Senga has a 9.00 ERA. The Cubs have already swept this team once. Go get it.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

research.facts:
Pre-Game Hype — Imanaga vs. Senga
**Facts:**
- First pitch: 6:10 PM CT at Citi Field, Tuesday June 23 (HIGH — series-context.json, multiple sources)
- Shota Imanaga 2026: 4-6, 4.26 ERA, 86.2 IP, 84 K, 1.062 WHIP (MEDIUM — single search; consistent with other references)
- Imanaga was originally scheduled for June 22 (rained out); tonight is his delayed start
- Cubs 3-0 vs. Mets in 2026 (April series sweep; HIGH — pipeline history)
- Imanaga's 2025 was his breakout MLB season (2.32 ERA in year 1)
**Sources:**
- SI.com, series-context.json (game details)
- CBS Sports, ESPN (Imanaga stats)
